2606.30096.title: Information Dynamics of Language Communication
2606.30096.paper_summary: Quantifying how meaning propagates through communicative exchanges remains underdeveloped in computational linguistics. Here we introduce an information-theoretic framework that quantifies the directed flow of semantic content between interlocutors and decomposes multi-source contributions into redundant, unique, and synergistic components. Our approach leverages large language models as probabilistic estimators of natural language to compute two measures: semantic transfer entropy (STE), which captures directed predictive influence between speakers, and semantic partial information decomposition (SPID), which resolves how multiple sources jointly shape a target's language. Across four experiments we show that the framework detects reduced information flow in cognitively rigid dialogue, captures the dominant role of persuaders in shaping discourse, distinguishes high- from low-quality psychotherapy by the directionality of therapist-client information exchange, and reveals synergistic premise contributions in argumentative essays. This framework opens new avenues for studying information dynamics in digital discourse, pedagogical interactions, clinical dialogues, and any domain in which the structure of linguistic exchange is of research relevance.
2606.30096.paper_summary_zh: 量化意義如何在交互交流中傳播在計算語言學中仍然發展不足。<lb/>在這裡，我們引入了一個信息理論框架，量化了交談者之間語義內容的有向流動，並將多來源的貢獻分解為冗餘、獨特和協同的組成部分。<lb/>我們的方法利用大型語言模型作為自然語言的概率估計器來計算兩個指標：語義轉移熵（STE），它捕捉了講者之間的有向預測影響，以及語義部分信息分解（SPID），它解析了多個來源如何共同塑造目標的語言。<lb/>在四個實驗中，我們展示了該框架能夠檢測到在認知僵化對話中減少的信息流，捕捉到說服者在塑造話語中的主導角色，通過治療師與客戶的信息交換的方向性來區分高質量與低質量的心理治療，並揭示了論證性文章中的協同前提貢獻。<lb/>這一框架為研究數字話語、教學互動、臨床對話以及任何語言交流結構具有研究相關性的領域中的信息動態開辟了新途徑。
2606.30096.author: Leonardo S. Goodall et.al.
2606.30096.authors: Leonardo S. Goodall, Andrea I. Luppi, Pedro A. M. Mediano
